page: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Daldsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It also includes a substantial 5669 GB local storage disk and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations place the D96alds_v7 in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes scoring in the top 10 percent, while large-block uncached reads rank in the top 25 percent. The platform demonstrates exceptional integer and compression capabilities, achieving top-tier results in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Database and key-value store performance are also outstanding, with Redis SET operations reaching 139.48 million operations per second and Passmark database operations ranking in the top 10 percent. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds for small and medium models reside in the top tier, though prompt processing for large 70B models drops to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96alds_v7 offers high compute density and massive local storage without the premium cost associated with high-memory configurations. The 1 to 2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a deliberate tradeoff, making it highly economical for CPU-bound tasks but less suitable for memory-intensive in-memory databases that require larger RAM footprints. The bundled 5669 GB local storage provides exceptional value for data-heavy operations. This server type is best suited for high-throughput web serving, compute-heavy batch processing, data compression, and database workloads that rely on fast local storage rather than massive RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 5669 GB local storage disk. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this x86_64 instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks place its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as Redis and database operations, in the top 10 percent tier. With a 1 to 2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it offers a cost-effective profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The server is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, data compression, and local storage-reliant database applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized platform pairing ninety-six dedicated AMD vCPUs with massive local storage for demanding data-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs
Includes 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a compute-optimized 1 to 2 resource ratio
Bundled with 5669 GB of high-capacity local storage
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU, database, and Redis operations
Well-suited for high-throughput web serving, data compression, and batch processing
